title: Federal and Bonded Funding Breakdown, Lead Inventory Scale, and Bureaucratic Barriers to Replacement
summary: Commissioner details funding sources: $2M one-time federal grant for lead service line inventory, $5M bonded in 2025 to continue replacements beyond pump station projects (added Westmoreland from Genesee to Euclid and other areas), $48K/year annual testing contract with Trupanti. JJ Lane contractor actively replacing lines -- any home with exceedance in sampling gets immediate replacement. Last testing round: 4 exceedances out of ~100 participants (20+ did not return kits despite prior participation). EPA requires homeowner self-testing (chain of custody: home to mail to lab, no intermediaries). Scale of remaining work: 14,000 known lead service lines (some public-side only, some private-side only) plus 16,000 unknown lines (concentrated in Eastwood and Salt Springs areas that were not part of the city when homes were built -- no records exist). Approximately 1,000 total replacements completed 2024-2025. Previous map of 2,900 private-side completions still in EFC review (Environmental Facilities Corporation, NY State) -- delayed from expected 2025 start. $25M CIP is for 2026 over 6 years (~$4.2M/year). Commissioner candidly identifies 'bureaucracy' as primary barrier to faster replacement -- contracts must go through law, council, and EFC approval. Ryan Or (water systems construction engineer) introduced during EFC discussion.

political_signal.description: Council pressing on replacement pace relative to 14,000+ known lead lines; commissioner identifies 'bureaucracy' (EFC, law, council processes) as primary barrier to faster execution

processing_notes: Department of Finance and Law Department in detected_departments are false positives from budget/contract discussion context; excluded
EFC = Environmental Facilities Corporation (NY State agency managing infrastructure financing) -- identified as bottleneck
Ryan Or = water systems construction engineer, introduced during EFC discussion
16,000 unknown service lines concentrated in Eastwood/Salt Springs -- areas annexed after homes built, no city records of service material
Commissioner initially says 'politics' then corrects to 'bureaucracy' as primary barrier
Segment 6 of 7 from Public Works and Infrastructure Committee Meeting, January 20th 2026
Potential duplicate detected: overlaps with CTX-COUNCIL-2026-00052 -- linked, not skipped
2026-03-21: Linked to CLUSTER-LEAD-REMEDIATION-2026Q1 (7 records from Jan 20 Public Works Committee lead water discussion).
